Summary of today's production run (Friday), second shift, for the Hershey's Zero Sugar product.
Overall, the production line has been operating satisfactorily, with no significant deviations noted during the inspections conducted thus far. However, it was observed that the product code on some units was partially misaligned—rendering it not fully legible—and that the flow-wrap packaging on certain units had come open. In light of this situation, personnel were instructed to remain vigilant and to intensify process monitoring to ensure the timely identification of any further deviations.
Additionally, a request was made to clean the trays used to collect the product during the transfer to the conveyor belt, thereby contributing to the maintenance of the final product's quality and presentation.

At the start of the shift, the Mixer Auger temperature was 104°F and the hopper temperature was 98.8°F. The hopper was full of chocolate, and chocolate was being deposited into the molds. The third-shift operator was informed of the importance of waiting for both the hoppers and the Water Temperature Control Unit to reach their established set points before starting the line, as well as the importance of completing the startup checklist prior to startup to verify that all line conditions and parameters are correct. The operator stated that she was not aware that she needed to wait for the temperatures to reach their set points before beginning production.

An opportunity for improvement was identified in the startup checklist. The checklist currently instructs operators to lower the hopper temperatures, but it does not specify that the temperatures must reach the set point before production can begin. Likewise, for the Water Temperature Control Unit, the checklist instructs operators to turn on the unit and the reference photo shows 94°F, but it does not specify that the set point should be adjusted to 93°F or that operators must wait for the unit to reach the set point before startup. Although these temperatures are defined in the centerlines, adding this verification to the startup checklist would help ensure that the proper conditions are met before starting the line.

Additionally, the importance of performing a test on a tray after any issue has been resolved or maintenance work has been completed on the depositor was reviewed with the operator. This test is necessary to verify that the deposit nozzles are not clogged before continuing production.
Filler ingredients check update

Since the line was started before the hopper and Water Temperature Control Unit reached their required set points, all molds deposited during third shift stuck to the molds. A total of 71 molds were affected and were sent to the dishwasher for cleaning. When first shift got to the line, production was stopped immediately. The lead and operator were informed, and the hopper was drained. The team waited until the required temperatures were reached, then the hoppers were refilled. A capture test was performed, and the line was restarted once the process was verified and all conditions were correct.

In the afternoon, machine number 5 was received with information from the outgoing operator that it was shutting down every two minutes and that it only needed to be restarted and turned back on. Indeed, it was shutting down every two minutes, but we informed the 🧰 mechanics and they found that the blue 🔵 band was incorrectly installed and the sensor was  being overworked, which is why the machine was shutting down. It was fixed and the machine has been ruining perfectly ever since. Kimberly Morales

Update: This recipe (21115 One Shot) is being made in Sollich 4 Kitchen for the first time.

We started cooking and will later check viscosity and water activity. Nathan Topham Justin Garner Charity Young Mary Montilla Katya Farnsworth Italu Martinez Curt Willis
Nathan Topham, will you get this info on the white board to track. Will you also put on the board the minimum and maximum please.

Hi Team,
The values for the first and second batches are posted on the whiteboard. I suggest lowering the temperature by 1 degree. Please let me know your thoughts.
Thank you. Justin Garner Nathan Topham
Chiller has faulted out in the kitchen maintenance has been informed
The water activity is around 0.40. If we lower the temperature by 1.5 degrees, we may be able to increase the water activity and reduce the viscosity.
I am waiting for approval.
We are going to cook another batch and watch the trend of the viscosity.
Italu Martinez Kathleen Gilbert Miriah Manzanares why would we want to increase water activity? I understand that the spec is 0.6-0.7.   but the current result is in a safe place.

As i understand, Aw will contribute to overall composition of the caramel in respect to moisture stickiness and textures. But, Italu Martinez Can you share more specifically the intent that the water activity must be within .6-.7? and thus, if it is below .6 it should be discarded?
After talking with Curt we are going to lower cooking to 220 and cook another batch. Curt Willis, Italu Martinez
It was 6 people 1 in the kitchen 1 at the slitter, two on the theegarten, and 2 packing
The HVAC tech will be out Monday morning to look at it. Chris Jeffs

We have not came across that low of water activity before so it's hard to say. The spec was set to that range because we never came across a water activity less than .64 during Princess (pilot cooker) and S3, S1 Trials. Though this is not a bad issue to have at the moment, we do not know what implications this may have to the caramel over the course of its shelf life. As Mariah mentioned, this can have an impact on texture. It may not be noticeable now but it could be months down the road (e.g.: we have nice loose caramel now but will it dry out within months?)
Can we please make sure we're gathering retains of this? This is something we will want to observe over time to make these decisions a little easier.
Justin Garner Curt Willis
Update: Please check the board. We are within specifications for 21115 One Shot, cooking at 220°F.👇

Hi Team,

The operator from Sollich 4 Kitchen called me and said that everything was showing red on the screen. I guided him step by step on how to recover the system and also explained how to finish the cooking process manually when the electrical power went out. Dakota Adair Curt Willis

Trend one One-Shot; viscosity has now gone too far down, and aW is up a little, but not in spec. 

However, cook temp has been the same for last 4 batches. 

We’re looking into this further now. 

Dakota Adair Curt Willis
Batch 7 Results: Water activity has risen slightly, but remains below the minimum; however, viscosity is very low—out of range.

I spoke with the operator, and he said he believes it is due to the power outage. 

Since he had to manually drop the caramel in D1 twice due to the power outage. 

The cook times also were higher than average for the batches he had to drop manually (29 min), instead of the average we saw previously 26(min)
Once we have this next batch that is being done under normal conditions we’ll have an even better idea.
Batch 8 Results: We continue with low viscosity.

We’re switching to kettle (D2). 

Since batch #8 was not being cooked/or manually sent during the power outage, which was the case for batch #6 and #7.
We need to add 453 grams of vanilla per batch.
That makes sense, I appreciate you being able to help troubleshoot the kitchen Marcos Jimenez
The problem is that the vanilla dispensing system became misconfigured. I instructed the operator to switch the reserve tank to manual mode and turned off the automatic dispensing.
It was dispensing too much vanilla due to the power interruption. That’s why it is better to keep it in manual mode.
Batch 9: We continue with low viscosity; we expect to see changes in the upcoming samples.
Just to let you know, the salt item 10002 did not arrive today afternoon, we hope that tomorrow will arrive.

Currently there’s only one person.

Summary of today's production run (Friday), second shift, for the Hershey's Zero Sugar product.
Overall, the production line has been operating satisfactorily, with no significant deviations noted during the inspections conducted thus far. However, it was observed that the product code on some units was partially misaligned—rendering it not fully legible—and that the flow-wrap packaging on certain units had come open. In light of this situation, personnel were instructed to remain vigilant and to intensify process monitoring to ensure the timely identification of any further deviations.
Additionally, a request was made to clean the trays used to collect the product during the transfer to the conveyor belt, thereby contributing to the maintenance of the final product's quality and presentation.
We only have one for lunches normally there is 2
We got an alarmed on HMI maintenance, QA, are notified
We started the line back up
Demolding, metal detector and C6 belts are running correctly
Demolding, metal detector and C6 belts are running correctly
Atp  magnet  ML1 passed
495 partial and erase
Shift 3 start-up verification: piece weight, organoleptic checks, flow wrap, packaging, and tempering—all within the correct range.
Maintenance is working on piston failure
C6, Metal detector, and demolding are running correctly
The piston not moving maintenance requires us to remove the piston and clean it
The depositor piston stopped moving. We are going to drain Inclusion hoper and depositor per maintenance request so they can troubleshoot the issue
Working on clogged ports
The line is currently down because one of the pistons is clogged; the plate has been disassembled for cleaning.
Clean plate, AT correct.
the plate is installed
No issues found is was a dirty pin
vdf1015 drive is faulting maintenance is working on it
Cavanna alarm on. QA, maintenance, and Supervisor are here to inspect
Line down Maintenance is still working on the issue
Still down
Maintenance says piston for cut off rack from one moves back piston doesn't move
Midshift
Piston actuator not moving piston
Maintenance, talked to Preston. They are trying to bring Nathan C over
Cleaning dosing head
Nozzles and dosing head cleaned
The plate and roller are washed again; ATP reading is correct.
Line still down maintenance working on the piston actuator

Frederick Peairs, Raoul Lubain,

Does it show a fault on the screen for this or just doesn’t move the pistons?

When we tight those completely , the piston doesn’t move, but if we keep it   a bit loose, the piston is able to move ..  we tried without chocolate  but now we are wating to fill the hopper  and  see if the piston can work .
Chocolate not flowing into the depositor maintenance is working on it
Filling now
Ml1 up and running
Great to hear. Let’s get some pictures around the depositor to ensure it isn’t leaking any chocolate out.

At the start of the shift, the Mixer Auger temperature was 104°F and the hopper temperature was 98.8°F. The hopper was full of chocolate, and chocolate was being deposited into the molds. The third-shift operator was informed of the importance of waiting for both the hoppers and the Water Temperature Control Unit to reach their established set points before starting the line, as well as the importance of completing the startup checklist prior to startup to verify that all line conditions and parameters are correct. The operator stated that she was not aware that she needed to wait for the temperatures to reach their set points before beginning production.

An opportunity for improvement was identified in the startup checklist. The checklist currently instructs operators to lower the hopper temperatures, but it does not specify that the temperatures must reach the set point before production can begin. Likewise, for the Water Temperature Control Unit, the checklist instructs operators to turn on the unit and the reference photo shows 94°F, but it does not specify that the set point should be adjusted to 93°F or that operators must wait for the unit to reach the set point before startup. Although these temperatures are defined in the centerlines, adding this verification to the startup checklist would help ensure that the proper conditions are met before starting the line.

Additionally, the importance of performing a test on a tray after any issue has been resolved or maintenance work has been completed on the depositor was reviewed with the operator. This test is necessary to verify that the deposit nozzles are not clogged before continuing production.
Filler ingredients check update

Since the line was started before the hopper and Water Temperature Control Unit reached their required set points, all molds deposited during third shift stuck to the molds. A total of 71 molds were affected and were sent to the dishwasher for cleaning. When first shift got to the line, production was stopped immediately. The lead and operator were informed, and the hopper was drained. The team waited until the required temperatures were reached, then the hoppers were refilled. A capture test was performed, and the line was restarted once the process was verified and all conditions were correct.
The piece of chocolate has an amount of almonds within the specified range.
